# The Singularity MCP Server

> The Unified OS for the Agentic Economy. One state root, four VMs, zero bridges.

## What This Is

This is the MCP (Model Context Protocol) server for The Singularity — an agent-native blockchain with 80 tools for on-chain operations. Connect from Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, or any MCP-compatible AI agent.

SSE endpoint: https://eto-mcp.fly.dev/sse
Health check: https://eto-mcp.fly.dev/health
Community: https://t.me/+MQ12Hel1E4hlZTQx

## Connect

### Claude Code
```bash
claude mcp add singularity --transport sse https://eto-mcp.fly.dev/sse
```

### Claude Desktop / Cursor / Windsurf
Add to your MCP config (claude_desktop_config.json or equivalent):
```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "singularity": {
      "url": "https://eto-mcp.fly.dev/sse"
    }
  }
}
```

### Any MCP Client
Connect via SSE transport to `https://eto-mcp.fly.dev/sse`. Messages are sent via POST to `/message?sessionId=<id>`.

## Authentication

Every `POST /message` requires an `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header. This server implements **OAuth 2.1 with PKCE** (RFC 9728 + RFC 8414 + RFC 7591) — the same pattern used by Linear, Figma, and Notion. Modern MCP clients handle the entire auth flow automatically.

Signing stays client-side via thirdweb SIWE. The server never touches your private key.

### Method 1 — Automatic via MCP client (recommended)

Modern MCP clients (Claude Code ≥1.x, Cursor, Windsurf, VS Code with MCP extension) detect the `WWW-Authenticate` header on the first 401 and trigger the full OAuth flow without any manual steps:

1. Client hits `/sse` → receives `401` with:
   ```
   WWW-Authenticate: Bearer realm="mcp", resource_metadata="https://eto-mcp.fly.dev/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource"
   ```
2. Client discovers the auth server via `/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource` and `/.well-known/oauth-authorization-server`
3. Client auto-registers via `POST /register` (Dynamic Client Registration, RFC 7591)
4. Client opens a browser tab to `/authorize` → you see the **thirdweb ConnectButton** (email, Google, Apple, MetaMask, WalletConnect, etc.)
5. Connect your wallet → sign once → browser tab closes
6. Client exchanges the auth code at `POST /token` (PKCE S256)
7. Bearer token is stored by the client and sent automatically on every call

**For Claude Code:**
```bash
claude mcp add singularity --transport sse https://eto-mcp.fly.dev/sse
# Browser opens automatically — connect wallet — done
```

**For Claude Desktop / Cursor / Windsurf** (add to your MCP config file):
```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "singularity": {
      "url": "https://eto-mcp.fly.dev/sse"
    }
  }
}
```
The client will trigger the browser-based auth flow on first connection.

**Login page (manual trigger):** https://eto-mcp.fly.dev/login

---

### Method 2 — Programmatic SIWE (scripts, agents, CI)

For headless environments where you can't open a browser. Uses `/auth/login` + `/auth/verify` directly.

**3-step handshake:**
```
1. POST /auth/login  {address, chainId}             → LoginPayload (EIP-4361)
2. client signs payload.message with wallet locally  → signature (0x...)
3. POST /auth/verify {payload, signature, strategy}  → {token, exp}
```

**Node / Bun example:**
```ts
import { createThirdwebClient } from "thirdweb";
import { signLoginPayload } from "thirdweb/auth";
import { privateKeyToAccount } from "thirdweb/wallets";

const BASE = "https://eto-mcp.fly.dev";
const CLIENT_ID = "<your thirdweb public client id>";
const client = createThirdwebClient({ clientId: CLIENT_ID });
const account = privateKeyToAccount({ client, privateKey: process.env.PRIVKEY! });

// Step 1: get payload
const payload = await fetch(`${BASE}/auth/login`, {
  method: "POST",
  headers: { "content-type": "application/json" },
  body: JSON.stringify({ address: account.address, chainId: 17743 }),
}).then(r => r.json());

// Step 2: sign
const { signature } = await signLoginPayload({ account, payload });

// Step 3: get token
const { token } = await fetch(`${BASE}/auth/verify`, {
  method: "POST",
  headers: { "content-type": "application/json" },
  body: JSON.stringify({ payload, signature, strategy: "siwe" }),
}).then(r => r.json());

// Use token as Bearer on /message calls
```

For in-app wallets (email, Google, Apple), use `inAppWallet({ auth: { options: ["email","google","apple"] } })` from `thirdweb/wallets` and set `strategy` to `"inapp_email"` or `"inapp_oauth"` on `/auth/verify`.

### Calling tools (JSON-RPC 2.0 over SSE)

1. Open `GET /sse` and read the first `event: endpoint` — it contains `data: /message?sessionId=<uuid>`. Keep the SSE stream open; tool results come back over it.
2. POST JSON-RPC to `/message?sessionId=<uuid>` with the Bearer:

```http
POST /message?sessionId=abc-123 HTTP/1.1
Host: eto-mcp.fly.dev
Authorization: Bearer eyJ...
Content-Type: application/json

{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"tools/call",
 "params":{"name":"list_wallets","arguments":{}}}
```

The POST returns HTTP 202 immediately; the actual JSON-RPC result arrives as a `message` event on the SSE stream keyed by the same `id`.

Common first calls: `session_info` (inspect what the server sees — scope, auth_strategy, wallets, active_wallet_id, last_restart_iso), `get_health`, `list_wallets`, `create_wallet`.

### Session lifetime & error shape

Tokens expire after **24 hours** (86400s). OAuth clients receive a `refresh_token` (valid 30 days) alongside the access token and can silently re-auth at `POST /token` with `grant_type=refresh_token`. Programmatic (SIWE) clients re-run the 3-call handshake on 401. Wallets are stored permanently on a persistent volume — re-authenticating with the same wallet address restores all your wallets.

Every auth error follows the MCP error envelope:
```json
{
  "code": "AUTH_001",
  "category": "auth",
  "message": "Authentication required",
  "explanation": "No Bearer token. Call POST /auth/login → sign → POST /auth/verify.",
  "recovery": [{"action":"authenticate","description":"..."}],
  "retryable": false
}
```

Codes you'll see: `AUTH_001` (missing/invalid/expired bearer), `AUTH_002` (insufficient capability for this tool), `AUTH_003` (tool not in the capability map).

### Capabilities & rate limits

Every tool is pinned to a capability scope (`wallet:read`, `token:write`, `deploy:write`, etc.). SIWE logins get the full default capability set; agent-delegated sessions can be issued with narrower scopes. Rate limits bucket requests into three tiers: `read`, `write`, `deploy`. Exceeding a bucket returns a 429 with `rate_limit` in the error category.

### Local / stdio mode

Running the server locally via `bun run start` (stdio transport) sets `AUTH_DEV_BYPASS=true` automatically and uses a single `"__stdio__"` scope — wallets persist to `~/.eto/wallets/__stdio__.enc`. The SSE/HTTP transport does **not** honor dev bypass unless you explicitly set `AUTH_DEV_BYPASS=true` in the environment; production always runs with bypass off.

### Verify the deploy yourself

```bash
# Health
curl -s https://eto-mcp.fly.dev/health
# → {"status":"ok","tools":81,"network":"testnet"}

# OAuth discovery
curl -s https://eto-mcp.fly.dev/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource
# → {"resource":"https://eto-mcp.fly.dev/","authorization_servers":[...],...}

curl -s https://eto-mcp.fly.dev/.well-known/oauth-authorization-server
# → {"issuer":"...","authorization_endpoint":".../authorize","token_endpoint":".../token","registration_endpoint":".../register",...}

# 401 with WWW-Authenticate (triggers OAuth flow in MCP clients)
curl -sI https://eto-mcp.fly.dev/sse
# → 401, WWW-Authenticate: Bearer realm="mcp", resource_metadata="..."

# Dynamic Client Registration
curl -s -X POST https://eto-mcp.fly.dev/register \
  -H 'content-type: application/json' \
  -d '{"client_name":"my-agent","redirect_uris":["http://127.0.0.1:9999/callback"],"grant_types":["authorization_code"],"response_types":["code"]}'
# → {"client_id":"...","client_secret":"...",...}
```

## Why This Exists

AI agents generate $30B+ in transaction volume but are locked to single chains. $600M+ in arbitrage sits on individual chains yearly. Cross-chain agents captured only $868M total — 50x untapped spreads sit across chains.

Existing chains weren't built for agents. Ethereum gas auctions cost $1-50+ per tx. Solana flat fees crush small trades. Every new "agent chain" is another island.

The Singularity: one state root, four VMs (SVM, EVM, WASM, Move) sharing it, zero bridges. Agents run as first-class programs with access to $150B+ in DeFi liquidity in a single transaction. 200ms settlement vs 242s industry average. Fees at 1-20bps, scaled by value.

## Architecture

- 3.4M+ TPS benchmarked
- 5,800+ agent tools accessible
- 500+ chains unified
- 200ms end-to-end settlement
- $0.0007 per signature, any size
- Two patents pending on multi-VM shared-state

## Tool Categories (80 tools)

### Wallets & Accounts
- `create_wallet` — Generate new wallet (SVM + EVM addresses)
- `import_wallet` — Import existing private key
- `get_wallet` — Get wallet details and balances
- `list_wallets` — List all managed wallets
- `set_active_wallet` — Set default wallet
- `get_balance` — Check native token balance

### Tokens
- `create_token` — Deploy new token with metadata
- `mint_tokens` — Mint tokens to address
- `burn_tokens` — Burn tokens from supply
- `transfer_token` — Transfer tokens between addresses
- `get_token_info` — Get token metadata and supply
- `get_token_balance` — Check token balance
- `list_token_holdings` — List all token holdings for address
- `freeze_token_account` — Freeze token account

### Transfers
- `transfer_native` — Send native ETO tokens
- `batch_transfer` — Send to multiple recipients in one tx
- `airdrop` — Request testnet tokens

### Smart Contracts
- `deploy_svm_program` — Deploy Solana-compatible program
- `deploy_evm_contract` — Deploy Solidity contract
- `deploy_wasm_contract` — Deploy WASM contract
- `deploy_move_module` — Deploy Move module
- `call_contract` — Call any deployed contract
- `read_contract` — Read contract state
- `get_contract_info` — Get contract metadata
- `encode_calldata` — ABI-encode function calls

### Cross-VM
- `cross_vm_call` — Call across VM boundaries in one tx
- `resolve_cross_vm_address` — Resolve address across VMs
- `derive_address` — Derive addresses between SVM/EVM

### Staking
- `create_stake` — Stake tokens with validator
- `deactivate_stake` — Begin unstaking
- `withdraw_stake` — Withdraw unstaked tokens
- `delegate_stake` — Delegate to validator
- `get_stake_info` — Get staking position details

### Agents (On-chain)
- `create_agent` — Deploy autonomous on-chain agent
- `execute_agent` — Trigger agent execution
- `get_agent` — Get agent details
- `list_agents` — List deployed agents
- `pause_agent` / `resume_agent` — Control agent execution
- `hire_agent` — Hire agent from marketplace
- `get_agent_reputation` — Get agent trust score

### Agent-to-Agent (A2A)
- `create_a2a_channel` — Open communication channel
- `send_a2a_message` — Send message between agents
- `read_a2a_messages` — Read channel messages
- `list_a2a_channels` — List open channels
- `close_a2a_channel` — Close channel

### Swarms
- `create_swarm` — Create multi-agent swarm
- `join_swarm` — Join existing swarm
- `get_swarm` — Get swarm state
- `swarm_propose` — Submit proposal to swarm
- `swarm_vote` — Vote on swarm proposal

### DAOs
- `create_dao` — Deploy DAO with governance
- `dao_propose` — Submit governance proposal
- `dao_vote` — Vote on proposal
- `dao_delegate` — Delegate voting power

### MCP Services (On-chain)
- `register_mcp_service` — Register MCP service on-chain
- `get_mcp_service` — Get service details
- `list_mcp_services` — Discover available services
- `call_mcp_service` — Call registered service

### Identity
- `register_agent_identity` — Register on-chain identity
- `discover_agents` — Search agent registry
- `manage_key_shares` — Manage distributed key shares

### Zero-Knowledge
- `zk_prove` — Generate Groth16 BN254 proof
- `zk_verify` — Verify proof on-chain
- `zk_bn254_ops` — Low-level BN254 curve operations (point_add, scalar_mul, pairing_check)

### Security & Policy
- `set_spending_limit` — Set transaction limits
- `set_address_whitelist` — Restrict allowed addresses
- `set_tool_permissions` — Control tool access per agent
- `create_policy_program` — Deploy custom policy logic
- `get_policy` — Get policy details
- `configure_step_up_auth` — Require additional auth for sensitive ops
- `configure_agent_trigger` — Set conditional agent triggers

### Subscriptions (Real-time)
- `subscribe_account` — Watch account changes
- `subscribe_blocks` — Stream new blocks
- `subscribe_logs` — Stream program logs
- `unsubscribe` — Stop subscription

### Queries
- `get_transaction` — Get transaction details
- `get_block` — Get block by height
- `get_block_height` — Get current block height
- `get_account` — Get account info
- `get_account_transactions` — Transaction history
- `get_health` — Chain health status
- `search` — Full-text search across chain
- `get_epoch_info` — Current epoch details

### Validators
- `list_validators` — List active validators
- `get_vote_accounts` — Get vote accounts

### Analytics
- `get_chain_stats` — Chain-wide statistics
- `get_gas_analytics` — Fee and gas analysis
- `get_portfolio` — Portfolio overview across tokens

### Batch Operations
- `batch_execute` — Multiple operations in one tx
- `batch_query` — Multiple reads in one call

### Intents & Templates
- `execute_intent` — Natural language transaction intent
- `plan_execution` — Preview execution plan
- `execute_template` — Run pre-built workflow
- `list_templates` — Available workflow templates

### Development
- `forge_compile` — Compile Solidity with Foundry
- `forge_create` — Compile and deploy in one step
- `cast_call` — Call contract via cast
- `cast_abi_encode` — Encode ABI calldata
- `anchor_init` — Initialize Anchor project
- `anchor_build` — Build Solana program
- `anchor_test` — Run Anchor tests

### Cross-chain Mesh
- `mesh_transfer` — Cross-chain transfer
- `mesh_verify` — Verify cross-chain proof
- `mesh_state` — Query mesh bridge state
- `mesh_history` — Cross-chain transfer history
- `mesh_attest_balance` — Attest balance for bridging

### Marketplace & EPL
- `get_activity_feed` — Agent activity stream
- `get_audit_log` — Audit trail
- `inspect_uth` — Inspect Universal Token Header
- `list_agent_services` — Browse agent marketplace

## Network

- Chain ID: 17743
- Native token: ETO (9 decimals)
- Network: testnet
- Fees: 1-20bps tiered by execution weight

BENQI, Dexalot, Quicknode, Circle, Octane, PAKT, Crossmint, Suzaku

- Ahyaan Sayed, CEO — $500M+ in transactions closed. Designed the original architecture.

- Ujjwal Singh, CPO — Avalanche Codebase Alum. Background in homomorphic encryption. Leads product and UX.
